title: Proclamation No. 695, s. 1941
text:
MALACAÑAN PALACE MANILA

BY THE PRESIDENT OF THE PHILIPPINES PROCLAMATION NO. 695

RESERVING FOR SCHOOL PURPOSES THREE PARCELS OF THE PUBLIC DOMAIN SITUATED IN THE BARRIO OF NAMALTUGAN AND POBLACION, RESPECTIVELY, MUNICIPAL DISTRICT OF BAYAG (SUB-PROVINCE OF APAYAO), MOUNTAIN PROVINCE, ISLAND OF LUZON.

Upon the recommendation of the Secretary of Agriculture and Commerce and pursuant to the provisions of section eighty-three of Commonwealth Act Numbered One hundred and forty-one, as amended, I hereby withdraw from sale or settlement and reserve for school purposes, under the administration of the Director of Education, subject to private rights, if any there be, the following parcels of the public domain, situated in the barrio of Namaltugan and Poblacion, respectively, municipal district of Bayag (sub-province of Apayao), Mountain Province, island of Luzon, and particularly described in Bureau of Lands plans Psu-109978 and Psu-110060, to wit:

(PSU-109978, Bo. of Namaltugan)

Beginning at a point marked 1 on Bureau of Lands Psu-109978, S.40-00’E. 20254.29m. more or less from Triangulation Station Mt. Mukmekeroyoi, thence

S. 75-34′ E. 268.89m. to point 2; S. 40-52′ W. 136.72m. to point 3; N. 85-52′ W. 242.74m. to point 4; N. 24-57′ E. 168.63m. to point 1, point of the beginning.

Containing n area of THIRTY FIVE THOUSAND FIVE HUNDRED EIGHTY NINE SQUARE METERS (35, 589), more or less.

Points 1, 2 and 4, P. L. S. Con. Mon.; point 3 G. I. S. on Tree.

Bounded on the Northeast, by Barrio Road; on Southeast, by Tabayogon River; on the South, by Old bed of the Tabayogon River; and on the Northwest, by property of Lorenzo Bautista.

Bearings true. Declination 0-23’E.

Points referred to are marked on Bureau of Lands plan Psu-109978.

Surveyed: June 27-28, 1938 & Oct. 17-18, 1939.

Approved: March 13, 1940.

(PSU-110060)

Lot No. 1.

Beginning at point marked 1 on Bureau of Land plan Psu-110060, N. 84-26′ E. 10699. 50. more or less from the Triangulation Station Mt. Mukmekeroyoi, thence

N. 49-10′ E. 126. 79m. to point 2; S. 40-52′ E. 140. 30m. to point 3; S. 38-59′ W. 93. 45m. to point 4, N. 53-23′ W. 160. 66m. to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of SIXTEEN THOUSAND THREE HUNDRED NINETY FIVE SQUARE METERS (16, 395), more or less.

Point 3, Nail on Tree; and the rest are P. L. S. Stone Mons.

Bounded on the Northeast, by Barrio Road; on the Southeast, by property of Mamerto R. Espiritu; on the Southwest, by Public Land; and on the Northwest, by property of the Municipal Government of Bayag (Presidencia Site).

Lot No. 2

Beginning at point marked 1 on Bureau of Lands Plan Psu-110060, N. 83-46′ E. 10919. 25m. more or less from Triangulation Station Mt. Mukmekeroyoi, thence

S. 34-01′ E. 159.14m. to point 2; S. 45-43′ W. 61.06m. to point 3; N. 32-56′ W. 72.17m. to point 4; S. 53-50′ W. 45.18m. to point 5; N. 40-27′ W. 76.23m. to point 6; N. 44-02′ E. 45.86m. to point 7; N. 44-01′ E. 69.06m. to point 1, point of the beginning.

Containing an area of THIRTEEN THOUSAND THREE HUNDRED FIFTY THREE SQUARE METERS (13, 353), more or less.

All corners are P. L. S. Stone Mons.

Bounded on the Northeast, by Public Land; on the Southeast, by properties of Pedro Bumat and the Municipal Government of Bayag (Presidencia Site); on the Southwest, by property of the Municipal Government of Bayag (Presidencia Site) and Barrio Road; and on the Northwest, by properties Memerto R. Espiritu and the Municipal Government of Bayag (Cemetery Site).

Bearings true. Declination 0-46′ E.

Points referred to are marked on Bureau if Lands plan Psu-110060.

Surveyed: June 24, 1938 – Oct. 22, 1939.

Approved: March 1, 1940.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Commonwealth of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done in the City of Manila, this 24 th day of March, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and forty-one, and of the Commonwealth of the Philippines, the sixth.

(Sgd.) MANUEL L. QUEZON

By the President:

(Sgd.) JORGE B. VARGAS Secretary to the President Source: Presidential Museum and Library
